content.problemStatement:
There are NNN people lost in a forest, and each person has a radio. The iii-th person's radio can transmit KiK_iKi‚Äč frequencies. Let the list of frequencies that the iii-th person can transmit be Fi=[f(i,1),f(i,2),‚Ä¶,f(i,Ki)]F_i = [f_{(i,1)}, f_{(i,2)}, \ldots, f_{(i, K_i)}]Fi‚Äč=[f(i,1)‚Äč,f(i,2)‚Äč,‚Ä¶,f(i,Ki‚Äč)‚Äč].

The iii-th person and jjj-th person can communicate with each other if **at least** one of the following holds true:


They can both transmit a common frequency i.e. (f(i,a)=f(j,b)f_{(i, a)} = f_{(j, b)}f(i,a)‚Äč=f(j,b)‚Äč for some a,ba, ba,b).
There exists a person kkk who can communicate with both iii-th person and jjj-th person.
You want to ensure that every pair of people can communicate with each other. To do so, you can perform the following operation:


You can transfer a frequency from one person's radio to another, i.e. select iii and jjj (1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N)(1 \le i, j \le N)(1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N),
delete a frequency xxx (present in FiF_iFi‚Äč) from FiF_iFi‚Äč, and append it to FjF_jFj‚Äč.
Determine the **minimum** number of operations to ensure that every pair of people can communicate with each other. If it is not possible, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the number of people
The next NNN lines describe the list of frequencies for each person.
The iii-th of these NNN lines contains an integer KiK_iKi‚Äč, the number of frequencies the radio of the iii-th person can transmit. This is followed by KiK_iKi‚Äč **unique** integers: f(i,1),f(i,2),‚Ä¶,f(i,Ki)f_{(i,1)}, f_{(i,2)}, \ldots, f_{(i, K_i)}f(i,1)‚Äč,f(i,2)‚Äč,‚Ä¶,f(i,Ki‚Äč)‚Äč, describing the list of frequencies for the iii-th person.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **minimum** number of operations to ensure that every pair of people can communicate with each other.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all testcases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105
Sum of ‚ąĎi=1NKi\sum_{i=1}^{N}K_i‚ąĎi=1N‚ÄčKi‚Äč over all testcases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: We can transfer frequency 555 from person 333 to person 111. We need only 111 operation in this case.
Test Case 2: We need a minimum of 333 operations. One possible sequence of operations is:

Transfer frequency 555 from person 222 to person 111
Transfer frequency 777 from person 444 to person 333
Transfer frequency 131313 from person 666 to person 555

Test Case 3: It can be shown that it's impossible to ensure that every pair of people can communicate with each other, using any number of operations. So the answer for this case is ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
